channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What to know about Carlo Acutis, the Catholic Church’s new millennial saint
item.description: Carlo Acutis will become the Catholic Church’s first millennial saint at a solemn canonization Mass on April 27 in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US, Japan, Philippines stage navy drills in disputed South China Sea as a Chinese ship keeps watch
item.description: ABOARD BRP JOSE RIZAL, South China Sea (AP) — The United States, Japan and the Philippines on Friday staged joint naval drills to boost crisis readiness off a disputed South China Sea shoal as a Chinese military ship kept watch from a distance.
item.dc:creator: AARON FAVILA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Myanmar at a glance: Embroiled in civil war, now facing more devastation after powerful earthquake
item.description: Even before a powerful 7.7-magnitude quake hit Myanmar on Friday, more than 3 million people in the country had been displaced, and hundreds of thousands were cut off from vital food and health programs as a result of a destructive four-year civil war that international groups claim has indiscrimina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israel on Friday launched an attack on Lebanon's capital for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war in November.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin says Trump plans for Greenland 'has nothing to do' with Russia
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says President Donald Trump's ambitions for Greenland has "nothing to do" with Russia as Vice President JD Vance lands to assess security base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fears of civil war in South Sudan stem from political rivalry between the president and his deputy
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Tensions are rising in the oil-rich east African nation of South Sudan after Riek Machar, who serves as one of the country’s vice presidents, was arrested in the capital.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European institutions target 'scientific asylum-seekers' from US
item.description: The move comes as federal funds for research are cut and studies are subject to ideological limitations.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, The Hill

item.title: South Korea's worst wildfires are now almost contained following rain and cooler weather
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The most destructive wildfires ever to hit South Korea were almost contained, authorities announced Friday, after rain and cooler temperatures helped fire crew put out the blazes that have killed 28 people and razed vast swaths of land since last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocks Thailand, Burma, collapsing buildings and killing more than 150
item.description: A massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake has caused buildings to collapse in Thailand and Burma, leaving more than 150 dead across both countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Powerful quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand and kills more than 150 people
item.description: A powerful earthquake has rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand, destroying buildings and bridges and killing at least 150 people, mostly in Myanmar.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance says Denmark has “underinvested” in Greenland’s security and demands Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump continues to talk of taking over the Danish territory.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grieshaber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil's lawyers appear in New Jersey court over jurisdiction of Columbia activist's case
item.description: Lawyers for a Columbia University graduate student who has become the face of President Donald Trump’s crackdown on campus protests against Israel appeared before a judge in Newark, New Jersey, to debate where his legal fight to be released from federal custody should play out.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australia to hold general elections on May 3 with inflation and a housing shortage major issues
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australians will go to the polls on May 3 for general elections with high costs of living and a shortage of housing likely weighing against the government as Prime Minister Anthony Albanese 's center-left Labor Party seeks a second three-year term.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World Central Kitchen says Israeli strike killed Gaza volunteer
item.description: The World Central Kitchen (WCK) said that an Israeli military strike killed one of the charity group’s volunteers working in the Gaza Strip and injured six other people. “Our hearts are heavy today as we mourn the loss of one of our volunteers in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says 'old relationship' with US 'is over' amid tension over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Thursday remarked on his country's relationship with the United States amid tensions over trade agreements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles admitted to hospital over side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: The United Kingdom’s King Charles III was admitted to the hospital Thursday due to unexpected side effects from cancer treatments.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US-Canada airline bookings plummet 70 percent: Report
item.description: Airline bookings between the U.S. and Canada are down by 70 percent compared to the same time last year, according to the aviation analytics company OAG.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US ramps up demands on Ukraine in latest mineral proposal
item.description: President Trump’s administration is ramping up demands on Ukraine in the latest proposal, looking to assume control of all of Kyiv’s critical energy and mineral resources as it pushes ahead to reach a ceasefire in the country’s three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Police use force to break up protests at a university in Turkey's capital
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Police used pepper spray, plastic pellets and water cannon against protesters in Turkey’s capital early Thursday, the latest clash in the country’s biggest anti-government protests in over a decade.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT BADENDIECK and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Sudan opposition says peace deal has collapsed after arrest of its leader
item.description: JUBA, South Sudan (AP) — South Sudan’s main opposition party said Thursday a peace agreement that ended a five-year civil war has collapsed, following the arrest of its leader Riek Machar a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: DENG MACHOL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea sent 3,000 more troops to Russia, according to South's assessment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea sent around 3,000 additional troops to Russia in January and February in continued support for Russian President Vladimir Putin’s war on Ukraine, South Korea’s military said Thursday in its latest assessment.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Houthis claim responsibility for strikes against US ships: report
item.description: The Houthis in Yemen claimed responsibility for alleged recent attacks against the USS Harry S. Truman aircraft carrier and U.S. warships in the Red Sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amanda Knox reveals surprising relationship with man who prosecuted her
item.description: Amanda Knox, convicted, then acquitted of murder, discusses her time in an Italian prison and what freedom means to her now.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Two military jets collide in midair stunt, crash to ground in fiery explosion
item.description: Two Alpha jets collided midair during a training exercise in France before crashing to the ground and exploding in a massive fireball, dramatic video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cruise passengers issued warning while passing through pirate hot spot
item.description: Passengers aboard the "Queen Anne" ship were issued an alarming warning while passing through a pirate hot spot.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US goes international for eggs amid bird flu outbreak
item.description: Imported eggs, typically used for pet food, are now being used in processed foods like cake mixes and salad dressings.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director attacked by Israeli settlers, detained by army
item.description: Co-director Basel Adra witnessed the detention and said around two dozen settlers — some masked, some carrying guns, some in Israeli uniform — attacked the village.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French actor Gérard Depardieu stands trial in Paris for alleged sexual assaults on film set
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French actor Gérard Depardieu went on trial Monday in Paris on charges of sexually assaulting two women on a movie set, in a case seen as a potential watershed for the #MeToo movement in France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli forces push deeper into Gaza and destroy its only cancer hospital
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli forces advanced deeper into the Gaza Strip on Friday and blew up the only specialized cancer hospital in the war-torn territory, as Israeli leaders vowed to capture more land until Hamas releases its remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: IBRAHIM HAZBOUN, SAM MEDNICK and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, WGN 9
